verses.sanskrit: उद्धव उवाचतत: स आगत्य पुरं स्वपित्रो-श्चिकीर्षया शं बलदेवसंयुत: ।निपात्य तुङ्गाद्रिपुयूथनाथंहतं व्यकर्षद् व्यसुमोजसोर्व्याम् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: uddhava uvācatataḥ sa āgatya puraṁ sva-pitrościkīrṣayā śaṁ baladeva-saṁyutaḥnipātya tuṅgād ripu-yūtha-nāthaṁhataṁ vyakarṣad vyasum ojasorvyām
verses.text: Śrī Uddhava said: Thereafter Lord Kṛṣṇa went to Mathurā City with Śrī Baladeva, and to please Their parents They dragged Kaṁsa, the leader of public enemies, down from his throne and killed him, pulling him along the ground with great strength.

verses.sanskrit: सान्दीपने: सकृत्प्रोक्तं ब्रह्माधीत्य सविस्तरम् ।तस्मै प्रादाद्वरं पुत्रं मृतं पञ्चजनोदरात् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: sāndīpaneḥ sakṛt proktaṁbrahmādhītya sa-vistaramtasmai prādād varaṁ putraṁmṛtaṁ pañca-janodarāt
verses.text: The Lord learned all the Vedas with their different branches simply by hearing them once from His teacher, Sāndīpani Muni, whom He rewarded by bringing back his dead son from the region of Yamaloka.

verses.sanskrit: समाहुता भीष्मककन्यया येश्रिय: सवर्णेन बुभूषयैषाम् ।गान्धर्ववृत्त्या मिषतां स्वभागंजह्रे पदं मूर्ध्नि दधत्सुपर्ण: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: samāhutā bhīṣmaka-kanyayā yeśriyaḥ savarṇena bubhūṣayaiṣāmgāndharva-vṛttyā miṣatāṁ sva-bhāgaṁjahre padaṁ mūrdhni dadhat suparṇaḥ
verses.text: Attracted by the beauty and fortune of Rukmiṇī, the daughter of King Bhīṣmaka, many great princes and kings assembled to marry her. But Lord Kṛṣṇa, stepping over the other hopeful candidates, carried her away as His own share, as Garuḍa carried away nectar.

verses.sanskrit: ककुद्मिनोऽविद्धनसो दमित्वास्वयंवरे नाग्नजितीमुवाह ।तद्भग्नमानानपि गृध्यतोऽज्ञा-ञ्जघ्नेऽक्षत: शस्त्रभृत: स्वशस्त्रै: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kakudmino ’viddha-naso damitvāsvayaṁvare nāgnajitīm uvāhatad-bhagnamānān api gṛdhyato ’jñāñjaghne ’kṣataḥ śastra-bhṛtaḥ sva-śastraiḥ
verses.text: By subduing seven bulls whose noses were not pierced, the Lord achieved the hand of Princess Nāgnijitī in the open competition to select her bridegroom. Although the Lord was victorious, His competitors asked the hand of the princess, and thus there was a fight. Well equipped with weapons, the Lord killed or wounded all of them, but He Himself was not hurt.

verses.sanskrit: प्रियं प्रभुर्ग्राम्य इव प्रियायाविधित्सुरार्च्छद् द्युतरुं यदर्थे ।वज्र्याद्रवत्तं सगणो रुषान्ध:क्रीडामृगो नूनमयं वधूनाम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: priyaṁ prabhur grāmya iva priyāyāvidhitsur ārcchad dyutaruṁ yad-arthevajry ādravat taṁ sa-gaṇo ruṣāndhaḥkrīḍā-mṛgo nūnam ayaṁ vadhūnām
verses.text: Just to please His dear wife, the Lord brought back the pārijāta tree from heaven, just as an ordinary husband would do. But Indra, the King of heaven, induced by his wives (henpecked as he was), ran after the Lord with full force to fight Him.

verses.sanskrit: सुतं मृधे खं वपुषा ग्रसन्तंदृष्ट्वा सुनाभोन्मथितं धरित्र्या ।आमन्त्रितस्तत्तनयाय शेषंदत्त्वा तदन्त:पुरमाविवेश ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sutaṁ mṛdhe khaṁ vapuṣā grasantaṁdṛṣṭvā sunābhonmathitaṁ dharitryāāmantritas tat-tanayāya śeṣaṁdattvā tad-antaḥ-puram āviveśa
verses.text: Narakāsura, the son of Dharitrī, the earth, tried to grasp the whole sky, and for this he was killed by the Lord in a fight. His mother then prayed to the Lord. This led to the return of the kingdom to the son of Narakāsura, and thus the Lord entered the house of the demon.

verses.sanskrit: तत्राहृतास्ता नरदेवकन्या:कुजेन दृष्ट्वा हरिमार्तबन्धुम् ।उत्थाय सद्यो जगृहु: प्रहर्ष-व्रीडानुरागप्रहितावलोकै: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrāhṛtās tā nara-deva-kanyāḥkujena dṛṣṭvā harim ārta-bandhumutthāya sadyo jagṛhuḥ praharṣa-vrīḍānurāga-prahitāvalokaiḥ
verses.text: There in the house of the demon, all the princesses kidnapped by Narakāsura at once became alert upon seeing the Lord, the friend of the distressed. They looked upon Him with eagerness, joy and shyness and offered to be His wives.

verses.sanskrit: आसां मुहूर्त एकस्मिन्नानागारेषु योषिताम् ।सविधं जगृहे पाणीननुरूप: स्वमायया ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: āsāṁ muhūrta ekasminnānāgāreṣu yoṣitāmsa-vidhaṁ jagṛhe pāṇīnanurūpaḥ sva-māyayā
verses.text: All those princesses were lodged in different apartments, and the Lord simultaneously assumed different bodily expansions exactly matching each and every princess. He accepted their hands in perfect rituals by His internal potency.

verses.sanskrit: तास्वपत्यान्यजनयदात्मतुल्यानि सर्वत: ।एकैकस्यां दश दश प्रकृतेर्विबुभूषया ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāsv apatyāny ajanayadātma-tulyāni sarvataḥekaikasyāṁ daśa daśaprakṛter vibubhūṣayā
verses.text: Just to expand Himself according to His transcendental features, the Lord begot in each and every one of them ten offspring with exactly His own qualities.

verses.sanskrit: कालमागधशाल्वादीननीकै रुन्धत: पुरम् ।अजीघनत्स्वयं दिव्यं स्वपुंसां तेज आदिशत् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: kāla-māgadha-śālvādīnanīkai rundhataḥ puramajīghanat svayaṁ divyaṁsva-puṁsāṁ teja ādiśat
verses.text: Kālayavana, the King of Magadha and Sālva attacked the city of Mathurā, but when the city was encircled by their soldiers, the Lord refrained from killing them personally, just to show the power of His own men.

verses.sanskrit: शम्बरं द्विविदं बाणं मुरं बल्वलमेव च ।अन्यांश्च दन्तवक्रादीनवधीत्कांश्च घातयत् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śambaraṁ dvividaṁ bāṇaṁmuraṁ balvalam eva caanyāṁś ca dantavakrādīnavadhīt kāṁś ca ghātayat
verses.text: Of kings like Śambara, Dvivida, Bāṇa, Mura, Balvala and many other demons, such as Dantavakra, some He killed Himself, and some He caused to be killed by others [Śrī Baladeva, etc.].

verses.sanskrit: अथ ते भ्रातृपुत्राणां पक्षयो: पतितान्नृपान् ।चचाल भू: कुरुक्षेत्रं येषामापततां बलै: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: atha te bhrātṛ-putrāṇāṁpakṣayoḥ patitān nṛpāncacāla bhūḥ kurukṣetraṁyeṣām āpatatāṁ balaiḥ
verses.text: Then, O Vidura, the Lord caused all the kings, both the enemies and those on the side of your fighting nephews, to be killed in the Battle of Kurukṣetra. All those kings were so great and strong that the earth seemed to shake as they traversed the warfield.

verses.sanskrit: सकर्णदु:शासनसौबलानांकुमन्त्रपाकेन हतश्रियायुषम् ।सुयोधनं सानुचरं शयानंभग्नोरुमूर्व्यां न ननन्द पश्यन् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa karṇa-duḥśāsana-saubalānāṁkumantra-pākena hata-śriyāyuṣamsuyodhanaṁ sānucaraṁ śayānaṁbhagnorum ūrvyāṁ na nananda paśyan
verses.text: Duryodhana was bereft of his fortune and duration of life because of the intricacy of ill advice given by Karṇa, Duḥśāsana and Saubala. When he lay on the ground with his followers, his thighs broken although he was powerful, the Lord was not happy to see the scene.

verses.sanskrit: कियान् भुवोऽयं क्षपितोरुभारोयद्‌द्रोणभीष्मार्जुनभीममूलै: ।अष्टादशाक्षौहिणिको मदंशै-रास्ते बलं दुर्विषहं यदूनाम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiyān bhuvo ’yaṁ kṣapitoru-bhāroyad droṇa-bhīṣmārjuna-bhīma-mūlaiḥaṣṭādaśākṣauhiṇiko mad-aṁśairāste balaṁ durviṣahaṁ yadūnām
verses.text: [After the end of the Battle of Kurukṣetra, the Lord said:] The abatement of the earth’s great burden, eighteen akṣauhiṇīs, has now been effected with the help of Droṇa, Bhīṣma, Arjuna and Bhīma. But what is this? There is still the great strength of the Yadu dynasty, born of Myself, which may be a more unbearable burden.

verses.sanskrit: मिथो यदैषां भविता विवादोमध्वामदाताम्रविलोचनानाम् ।नैषां वधोपाय इयानतोऽन्योमय्युद्यतेऽन्तर्दधते स्वयं स्म ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: mitho yadaiṣāṁ bhavitā vivādomadhv-āmadātāmra-vilocanānāmnaiṣāṁ vadhopāya iyān ato ’nyomayy udyate ’ntardadhate svayaṁ sma
verses.text: When they quarrel among themselves, influenced by intoxication, with their eyes red like copper because of drinking [madhu], then only will they disappear; otherwise, it will not be possible. On My disappearance, this incident will take place.

verses.sanskrit: एवं सञ्चिन्त्य भगवान् स्वराज्ये स्थाप्य धर्मजम् ।नन्दयामास सुहृद: साधूनां वर्त्म दर्शयन् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ sañcintya bhagavānsva-rājye sthāpya dharmajamnandayām āsa suhṛdaḥsādhūnāṁ vartma darśayan
verses.text: Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, thus thinking to Himself, established Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira in the position of supreme control of the world in order to show the ideal of administration on the path of piety.

verses.sanskrit: उत्तरायां धृत: पूरोर्वंश: साध्वभिमन्युना ।स वै द्रौण्यस्त्रसंप्लुष्ट: पुनर्भगवता धृत: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: uttarāyāṁ dhṛtaḥ pūrorvaṁśaḥ sādhv-abhimanyunāsa vai drauṇy-astra-sampluṣṭaḥpunar bhagavatā dhṛtaḥ
verses.text: The embryo of Pūru’s descendant begotten by the great hero Abhimanyu in the womb of Uttarā, his wife, was burnt by the weapon of the son of Droṇa, but later he was again protected by the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: अयाजयद्धर्मसुतमश्वमेधैस्त्रिभिर्विभु: ।सोऽपि क्ष्मामनुजै रक्षन् रेमे कृष्णमनुव्रत: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ayājayad dharma-sutamaśvamedhais tribhir vibhuḥso ’pi kṣmām anujai rakṣanreme kṛṣṇam anuvrataḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Lord induced the son of Dharma to perform three horse sacrifices, and Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, constantly following Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead, protected and enjoyed the earth, assisted by his younger brothers.

verses.sanskrit: भगवानपि विश्वात्मा लोकवेदपथानुग: ।कामान् सिषेवे द्वार्वत्यामसक्त: सांख्यमास्थित: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavān api viśvātmāloka-veda-pathānugaḥkāmān siṣeve dvārvatyāmasaktaḥ sāṅkhyam āsthitaḥ
verses.text: Simultaneously, the Personality of Godhead enjoyed life in the city of Dvārakā, strictly in conformity with the Vedic customs of society. He was situated in detachment and knowledge, as enunciated by the Sāṅkhya system of philosophy.

verses.sanskrit: स्‍निग्धस्मितावलोकेन वाचा पीयूषकल्पया ।चरित्रेणानवद्येन श्रीनिकेतेन चात्मना ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: snigdha-smitāvalokenavācā pīyūṣa-kalpayācaritreṇānavadyenaśrī-niketena cātmanā
verses.text: He was there in His transcendental body, the residence of the goddess of fortune, with His usual gentle and sweetly smiling face, His nectarean words and His flawless character.

verses.sanskrit: इमं लोकममुं चैव रमयन् सुतरां यदून् ।रेमे क्षणदया दत्तक्षणस्त्रीक्षणसौहृद: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: imaṁ lokam amuṁ caivaramayan sutarāṁ yadūnreme kṣaṇadayā datta-kṣaṇa-strī-kṣaṇa-sauhṛdaḥ
verses.text: The Lord enjoyed His pastimes, both in this world and in other worlds [higher planets], specifically in the association of the Yadu dynasty. At leisure hours offered by night, He enjoyed the friendship of conjugal love with women.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यैवं रममाणस्य संवत्सरगणान् बहून् ।गृहमेधेषु योगेषु विराग: समजायत ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyaivaṁ ramamāṇasyasaṁvatsara-gaṇān bahūngṛhamedheṣu yogeṣuvirāgaḥ samajāyata
verses.text: The Lord was thus engaged in household life for many, many years, but at last His detachment from ephemeral sex life was fully manifested.

verses.sanskrit: दैवाधीनेषु कामेषु दैवाधीन: स्वयं पुमान् ।को विश्रम्भेत योगेन योगेश्वरमनुव्रत: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: daivādhīneṣu kāmeṣudaivādhīnaḥ svayaṁ pumānko viśrambheta yogenayogeśvaram anuvrataḥ
verses.text: Every living entity is controlled by a supernatural force, and thus his sense enjoyment is also under the control of that supernatural force. No one, therefore, can put his faith in Lord Kṛṣṇa’s transcendental sense activities but one who has become a devotee of the Lord by rendering devotional service.

verses.sanskrit: पुर्यां कदाचित्क्रीडद्‍‌भिर्यदुभोजकुमारकै: ।कोपिता मुनय: शेपुर्भगवन्मतकोविदा: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: puryāṁ kadācit krīḍadbhiryadu-bhoja-kumārakaiḥkopitā munayaḥ śepurbhagavan-mata-kovidāḥ
verses.text: Once upon a time, great sages were made angry by the sporting activities of the princely descendants of the Yadu and Bhoja dynasties, and thus, as desired by the Lord, the sages cursed them.

verses.sanskrit: तत: कतिपयैर्मासैर्वृष्णिभोजान्धकादय: ।ययु: प्रभासं संहृष्टा रथैर्देवविमोहिता: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ katipayair māsairvṛṣṇi-bhojāndhakādayaḥyayuḥ prabhāsaṁ saṁhṛṣṭārathair deva-vimohitāḥ
verses.text: A few months passed, and then, bewildered by Kṛṣṇa, all the descendants of Vṛṣṇi, Bhoja and Andhaka who were incarnations of demigods went to Prabhāsa, while those who were eternal devotees of the Lord did not leave but remained in Dvārakā.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र स्‍नात्वा पितृन्देवानृषींश्चैव तदम्भसा ।तर्पयित्वाथ विप्रेभ्यो गावो बहुगुणा ददु: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra snātvā pitṝn devānṛṣīṁś caiva tad-ambhasātarpayitvātha viprebhyogāvo bahu-guṇā daduḥ
verses.text: After arriving there, all of them took bath, and with the water of this place of pilgrimage they offered their respects to the forefathers, demigods and great sages and thus satisfied them. They gave cows to the brāhmaṇas in royal charity.

verses.sanskrit: हिरण्यं रजतं शय्यां वासांस्यजिनकम्बलान् ।यानं रथानिभान् कन्या धरां वृत्तिकरीमपि ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: hiraṇyaṁ rajataṁ śayyāṁvāsāṁsy ajina-kambalānyānaṁ rathān ibhān kanyādharāṁ vṛtti-karīm api
verses.text: The brāhmaṇas were not only given well-fed cows in charity, but also gold, gold coins, bedding, clothing, animal-skin seats, blankets, horses, elephants, girls and sufficient land for maintenance.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं चोरुरसं तेभ्यो दत्त्वा भगवदर्पणम् ।गोविप्रार्थासव: शूरा: प्रणेमुर्भुवि मूर्धभि: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: annaṁ coru-rasaṁ tebhyodattvā bhagavad-arpaṇamgo-viprārthāsavaḥ śūrāḥpraṇemur bhuvi mūrdhabhiḥ
verses.text: Thereafter they offered the brāhmaṇas highly delicious foodstuffs first offered to the Personality of Godhead and offered their respectful obeisances by touching their heads to the ground. They lived perfectly by protecting the cows and the brāhmaṇas.
